functions.description: Initializes the contract.  Must be called exactly once.  * `admin`      — address authorised to call administrative functions. * `rp_id_hash` — SHA-256 of the relying party identifier string (e.g., `sha256(b"vitastellar.health")`).

functions.name: issue_registration_challenge
functions.description: Issues a registration challenge for `user`.  The 32-byte challenge must be embedded in `clientDataJSON.challenge` during the FIDO2 attestation ceremony.  Valid for 5 minutes.

functions.name: issue_auth_challenge
functions.description: Issues a one-time authentication challenge for `user`.

functions.name: get_device_count
functions.description: Returns the total device count (active + revoked) for `user`.

functions.name: get_active_device_count
functions.description: Returns the number of active (non-revoked) devices for `user`.

functions.name: is_device_registered
functions.params: env: Env, user: Address, credential_id_hash: BytesN<32>
functions.description: Returns `true` if `credential_id_hash` is registered and active for `user`.

functions.name: mul_bps
functions.description: Multiply `amount` by basis points (1 bps = 0.01%) using floor division.  Floor rounding ensures fees are never rounded up — the fee taker always receives ≤ the exact fractional amount. Callers can reconstruct the complementary side as `amount - fee` to guarantee `fee + remainder == amount`.  Returns `None` if the intermediate `amount * bps` overflows `i128`.

functions.name: mul_bps_round_half_up
functions.description: Multiply `amount` by basis points with round-half-up rounding.  Returns `None` on overflow.

functions.name: set_bal
functions.params: env: Env, user: Address, amount: i128

functions.name: add_verifier
functions.description: Add a verifier (only owner can do this).  SECURITY (issue #43): the previously-blind `assign_role(Staff)` call has been guarded so that a verifier who already holds a higher-privileged role (Admin, Doctor, Researcher) keeps that role untouched. Only verifiers without any of those higher roles receive the `Staff` marker. Either way, the local `Verifier(addr) -> true` flag is set so the contract-level verifier registry stays consistent.  Trade-off (intentional): if a verifier was originally added while only holding `Staff` and is later promoted to a higher role (Admin/Doctor /Researcher) without an intervening `remove_verifier`, the `Staff` row will remain in RBAC. `remove_verifier` will then leave it alone because of the higher-role guard. Operators that need the row removed should call `remove_verifier` before the promotion.

functions.name: remove_verifier
functions.description: Remove a verifier (only owner can do this).  SECURITY (issue #43): as with `add_verifier`, the `remove_role(Staff)` call is now skipped whenever the target already holds a higher-privileged role (Admin, Doctor, Researcher). Stripping `Staff` from those users could be misinterpreted as a privilege revocation and risks disturbing the higher-privileged role state, so the call is intentionally a no-op in that case. The local `Verifier(addr)` flag is always cleared.  Trade-off (intentional, mirrors `add_verifier`): if a verifier was originally added while only holding `Staff` and was later promoted to a higher role, the pre-existing `Staff` row is preserved by this function alongside the higher role. To clear `Staff` from such an address, demote it back to non-staff roles first.

functions.description: Mark a single notification as Read. Only the recipient may call this.

functions.name: mark_all_read
functions.description: Mark all Pending / Delivered notifications for the caller as Read. Returns the count of newly-read notifications.

functions.name: archive_notification
functions.params: env: Env, caller: Address, notif_id: u64
functions.description: Archive a notification so it no longer appears in default queries. Caller must be the recipient or admin.

functions.name: delete_alert_rule
functions.params: env: Env, caller: Address, rule_id: u64
functions.description: Permanently delete an alert rule.

functions.name: get_alert_rules
functions.description: Returns all non-deleted alert rules. Admin only.

functions.name: get_analytics
functions.description: Returns aggregated send/read/pending counters. Admin only.

functions.description: Release the per-contract reentrancy lock.

functions.name: sanitize_string
functions.description: Validates a general-purpose string: non-empty, within `max_len` bytes, no null bytes, no ASCII control characters (allows tab/LF/CR).

functions.name: sanitize_name
functions.description: Validates a human name: letters (any UTF-8), digits, spaces, hyphens, apostrophes, commas, and periods only (ASCII subset).

functions.name: sanitize_email
functions.description: Validates an email address: single '@', non-empty local and domain parts, domain contains at least one '.', all chars from the RFC 5321 allowed set.

functions.name: sanitize_id
functions.description: Validates an identifier: alphanumeric chars, hyphens, underscores, colons, dots, and forward slashes (covers DIDs, slugs, and resource paths).

functions.name: sanitize_url
functions.description: Validates a URL: printable ASCII only, length within MAX_URL_LEN.

functions.name: initialize
functions.description: Initialize enclave infrastructure with a genesis signing key.

functions.name: rotate_signing_key
functions.params: env: Env, new_key: BytesN<32>, grace_period_seconds: u64
functions.description: Performs atomic key rotation using a programmatic expiration cutoff window.

functions.name: verify_signature
functions.description: Validates payload cryptographic authenticity across active key state boundaries.

functions.name: housekeeping
functions.description: Housekeeping endpoint used to explicitly clear old records and save storage fees.

functions.description: Resolves an existing registered Enclave Node data record.

functions.description: Resolves an existing assigned Processing Task details record.
